mnemonics.title: PDA Components Song
mnemonics.content: Q (states), Sigma (input), Gamma (stack), delta (transition), q-zero (start), Z-zero (stack start), F (final) - All together make PDA great!

mnemonics.title: Stack LIFO Reminder
mnemonics.content: Last In First Out - Think of a stack of plates: you can only add or remove from the TOP, like a stack of plates you wash.

mnemonics.title: Acceptance Methods
mnemonics.content: FES (Final Empty State) - Final state or Empty stack, both end in S for Success in accepting strings.

acronyms.usage: A finite automaton with an added stack memory for recognizing context-free languages.
A PDA that can have multiple possible transitions from a configuration, recognizes all CFLs.
A PDA with at most one move per configuration, recognizes deterministic CFLs (a proper subset).
Languages recognized by NPAs, characterized by nested dependencies and balanced structures.
Triple (q, w, α) representing current state, remaining input, and stack contents.
Stack access discipline where the most recently added element is removed first.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a vertical stack of books. You can only: 1) Place a new book on top (push), 2) Remove the top book (pop), or 3) Replace the top book with several others. The bottom book Z₀ stays until the end.
Draw three columns: Current State | Remaining Input | Stack (vertical with top at right). This shows the complete snapshot of PDA at any moment.
Think of a fork in the road where the PDA tries ALL paths simultaneously. If ANY path leads to acceptance, the string is accepted.
(q, a, X) → (p, γ): Like a recipe - FROM state q, READ 'a', LOOK at X on top, GO to state p, REPLACE X with γ.

analogies.analogy: Like a stack of plates in a cafeteria - you add plates to the top and remove from the top. You can't grab a plate from the middle without removing those above it first.
Like taking a breath while reading - you pause (don't consume characters) but your mind can reorganize thoughts (change state/stack).
Finite Automaton has the memory of a goldfish (fixed states), PDA has the memory of an elephant (unbounded stack) but only remembers in reverse order (LIFO).
Like clearing a desk completely - when all papers (stack symbols except Z₀) are gone, the task is complete.
